{"society_power": 7.0, "state_power": 8.5, "society_change": -1.0, "state_change": 1.0, "key_event": "WWI mobilization and Union sacr\u00e9e expand the wartime state", "reasoning": "Total-war mobilization, the income tax, three-year conscription, war-economy direction, and Clemenceau's centralization dramatically enlarged state capacity, while the Union sacr\u00e9e, Jaur\u00e8s's assassination, and suppression of the 1917 unrest temporarily subordinated an otherwise dense civil society. Society remains robust (1917 mutinies/strikes show it was thwarted, not destroyed), but its wartime self-subordination pulls its effective power down from its Belle \u00c9poque peak."}
